# Compliance API
####  Compliance APIActivities
##### [Query compliance activities](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance/activities/list)
GET/v1/compliance/activities
##### ModelsExpand Collapse 
ActivityListResponse = object { actor, decision, id, 5 more }  or object { actor, id, created_at, 3 more }  or object { actor, admin_api_key_id, scopes, 5 more }  or 326 moreî‚š
An external anti-abuse service reported a consequential decision about a sign-in or sign-up attempt.
AbuseDecisionReceived object { actor, decision, id, 5 more } î‚š
An external anti-abuse service reported a consequential decision about a sign-in or sign-up attempt.
actor: object { api_key_id, ip_address, user_agent, type }  or object { email_address, ip_address, user_agent, 2 more }  or object { ip_address, user_agent, type, unauthenticated_email_address }  or 5 moreî‚š
A federated external workload authenticated via a verified OIDC token.
Carries the verified issuer, subject, and audience claims from the presented JWT.

Whether code review was enabled before the change. Absent when no configuration existed before this update.

previous_model: optional stringî‚š

previous_per_review_limit_usd: optional stringî‚š

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B29%5D.previous_per_review_limit_usd)

FederatedIdentityActor object { issuer, subject, audience, 3 more } î‚š
A federated external workload authenticated via a verified OIDC token.
Carries the verified issuer, subject, and audience claims from the presented JWT.
issuer: stringî‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.actor%5B7%5D.issuer)
subject: stringî‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.actor%5B7%5D.subject)
audience: optional array of stringî‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.actor%5B7%5D.audience)
ip_address: optional stringî‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.actor%5B7%5D.ip_address)
type: optional "federated_identity_actor"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.actor%5B7%5D.type)
user_agent: optional stringî‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.actor%5B7%5D.user_agent)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.actor%5B7%5D)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.actor)
claude_project_id: stringî‚š
Tagged ID of the project the sync source belongs to.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.claude_project_id)
claude_project_sync_source_id: stringî‚š
Tagged ID of the per-project sync source that was updated.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.claude_project_sync_source_id)
provider: stringî‚š
The external provider backing the sync source, e.g. `github`, `google_drive`, `outline`, `slack`, `salesforce`, `google_calendar`, `gmail`, `asana`, or `mcp_resources`.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.provider)
id: optional stringî‚š
Unique identifier for the activity e.g. 'activity_abcd1234'
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.id)
config_changed: optional booleanî‚š
Whether the update changed the stored sync-source configuration, including sync settings such as path filters. False for a re-sync or a metadata-only refresh of the same resource.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.config_changed)
created_at: optional stringî‚š
When this activity occurred.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.created_at)
organization_id: optional stringî‚š
Organization ID this activity is associated with
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.organization_id)
organization_uuid: optional stringî‚š
Organization UUID where the activity occurred. Null when the activity is not tied to an organization (for example, login and logout events or calls to the Compliance API).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.organization_uuid)
resource_descriptor: optional stringî‚š
A short provider-specific identifier for the external resource after the update, e.g. `owner/repo` for GitHub or a file ID for Google Drive.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.resource_descriptor)
type: optional "claude_project_sync_source_updated"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B48%5D)

How the project was created: "direct", "duplicate", "remix", or "template_from_project".

The Design project that was created, e.g. "design_proj_01HX...".
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B54%5D.design_project_id)

Unique identifier for the activity e.g. 'activity_abcd1234'

organization_uuid: optional stringî‚š
Organization UUID where the activity occurred. Null when the activity is not tied to an organization (for example, login and logout events or calls to the Compliance API).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B54%5D.organization_uuid)
project_type: optional stringî‚š
The project type: "project", "template", or "design_system". May be unset for projects created by duplicating or remixing another project.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B54%5D.project_type)

The source project this was created from, when created via duplicate, remix, or template-from-project. Unset for direct creation.

The OpenTelemetry export endpoint after the change. Credentials in the URL userinfo or query string are removed; path segments are retained. Null if the endpoint is unset or was not itself modified by this update.

new_otlp_resource_attributes: optional stringî‚š

otlp_headers_change: optional "cleared" or "set"î‚š
Whether the OpenTelemetry export headers were set or cleared. 'set' is recorded for any non-empty submission, including resubmission of an unchanged value. Header values are never included.
"cleared"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B133%5D.otlp_headers_change%5B0%5D)

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B133%5D.otlp_headers_change)
previous_otlp_endpoint: optional stringî‚š

Plugin name
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B246%5D.plugin_name)

Unique identifier for the activity e.g. 'activity_abcd1234'
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B246%5D.id)
action: optional stringî‚š
Action taken (e.g. 'deleted' for clearing an override)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B246%5D.action)
created_at: optional stringî‚š
When this activity occurred.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B246%5D.created_at)
group_id: optional stringî‚š
Tagged group ID for group-level overrides (null for org-level)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B246%5D.group_id)
group_name: optional stringî‚š
Group name for group-level overrides
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B246%5D.group_name)
installation_preference: optional stringî‚š
New installation preference value (set only when action is an update; null for delete actions)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B246%5D.installation_preference)
organization_id: optional stringî‚š
Organization ID this activity is associated with
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B246%5D.organization_id)
organization_uuid: optional stringî‚š
Organization UUID where the activity occurred. Null when the activity is not tied to an organization (for example, login and logout events or calls to the Compliance API).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B246%5D.organization_uuid)

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#activity_list_response%5B246%5D.type)

The second authentication factor performed during this login, if any. `null` when the second-factor status is not recorded on this event â€” for example, when authentication was delegated to an external identity provider and any second factor is not visible to Anthropic, or when this event is one step of a multi-step login whose MFA is reported on another activity. May be absent on activities recorded before this field was introduced.

TunnelTokenRotated object { actor, tunnel_id, tunnel_token_id, 6 more } î‚š
The Cloudflare connector secret for a tunnel was rotated.
`tunnel_token_id` is the id of the _newly-issued_ token. The previous token is invalidated by the rotation and its id is not recorded here.
actor: object { api_key_id, ip_address, user_agent, type }  or object { email_address, ip_address, user_agent, 2 more }  or object { ip_address, user_agent, type, unauthenticated_email_address }  or 5 moreî‚š
A federated external workload authenticated via a verified OIDC token.
Carries the verified issuer, subject, and audience claims from the presented JWT.

####  Compliance APIOrganizations
##### [List organizations](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance/organizations/list)
GET/v1/compliance/organizations
##### ModelsExpand Collapse 
OrganizationListResponse object { data } î‚š
List of organizations under a parent organization.
data: array of object { created_at, name, uuid } î‚š
List of organizations sorted by creation date, ascending
created_at: stringî‚š
Organization creation time (RFC 3339 format)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#organization_list_response.data.items.created_at)
name: stringî‚š
Organization name
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#organization_list_response.data.items.name)
uuid: stringî‚š
Unique identifier for the organization (UUID format)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#organization_list_response.data.items.uuid)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#organization_list_response.data)

####  Compliance APIOrganizationsUsers
##### [List organization users](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance/organizations/users/list)
GET/v1/compliance/organizations/{org_uuid}/users
##### ModelsExpand Collapse 
UserListResponse object { id, created_at, email, 2 more } î‚š
User member information for compliance responses.
User identifier (tagged ID)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#user_list_response.id)
created_at: stringî‚š
User account creation timestamp
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#user_list_response.created_at)
email: stringî‚š
User's current email address
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#user_list_response.email)
full_name: stringî‚š
User's current full name
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#user_list_response.full_name)
organization_role: "admin" or "billing" or "claude_code_user" or 6 moreî‚š
User's built-in role within the organization. This is distinct from any custom RBAC roles that may also be assigned.
"admin"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#user_list_response.organization_role%5B0%5D)
"billing"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#user_list_response.organization_role%5B1%5D)

####  Compliance APIOrganizationsSettings
##### [Get effective organization settings](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance/organizations/settings/retrieve)
GET/v1/compliance/organizations/{organization_id}/settings
##### ModelsExpand Collapse 
SettingRetrieveResponse object { api_keys, organization_id, settings, type } î‚š
The resolved settings in force for one organization at read time.
Settings appear at most once each, in a fixed relative order, and values reflect the enforced state. A setting the organization's administrators cannot change â€” for example, one controlled by Anthropic policy or not available to the organization â€” is omitted from the list.
api_keys: array of object { id, created_at, created_by_id, 4 more } î‚š
Compliance API keys configured for the organization hierarchy, ordered by creation time ascending. Key secret values are never included.
Unique identifier for the API key.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.api_keys.items.id)
created_at: stringî‚š
When the key was created.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.api_keys.items.created_at)
created_by_id: stringî‚š
Identifier of the user who created the key, or null when the key was created by automation or its creator's account no longer exists.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.api_keys.items.created_by_id)
is_active: booleanî‚š
Whether the key is currently active. A deactivated key is listed for audit visibility but cannot authenticate requests.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.api_keys.items.is_active)
name: stringî‚š
The name given to the API key when it was created.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.api_keys.items.name)
scopes: array of stringî‚š
The permission scopes granted to the key.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.api_keys.items.scopes)
type: optional "compliance_api_key"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.api_keys.items.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.api_keys)

ProvisioningMode object { value, name, type } î‚š
How organization members are provisioned, resolved to the enforced mode.
A configured mode is reported only while the mechanism that enforces it is active: just-in-time modes require single sign-on to be enabled, and SCIM modes require directory sync to be enabled. Otherwise `login_only` is reported, regardless of any stored configuration.
value: "jit_advanced" or "jit_permissive" or "login_only" or 2 moreî‚š
How organization members are provisioned under SSO.
"jit_advanced"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.settings.items%5B3%5D.value%5B0%5D)
"jit_permissive"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.settings.items%5B3%5D.value%5B1%5D)
"login_only"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.settings.items%5B3%5D.value%5B2%5D)
"scim_advanced"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.settings.items%5B3%5D.value%5B3%5D)
"scim_permissive"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.settings.items%5B3%5D.value%5B4%5D)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.settings.items%5B3%5D.value)
name: optional "sso_provisioning_mode"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.settings.items%5B3%5D.name)
type: optional "provisioning_mode"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.settings.items%5B3%5D.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.settings.items%5B3%5D)
DataRetention object { value, name, type } î‚š
The data retention periods in force, keyed by the type of data they apply to.
A key of `all` covers every data type and is exclusive: when present it is the only key. A missing key means no organization-level administrator-configured retention period is in force for that data type; Anthropic's service defaults may still apply.
value: map[object { duration, timescale, type }  or object { type } ]î‚š
Fixed object { duration, timescale, type } î‚š
A fixed retention window measured from each item's last activity.
duration: numberî‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.settings.items%5B4%5D.value.items%5B0%5D.duration)
timescale: "day" or "month"î‚š
"day"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.settings.items%5B4%5D.value.items%5B0%5D.timescale%5B0%5D)
"month"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.settings.items%5B4%5D.value.items%5B0%5D.timescale%5B1%5D)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.settings.items%5B4%5D.value.items%5B0%5D.timescale)
type: optional "fixed"î‚š

[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#setting_retrieve_response.settings.items%5B4%5D.value.items%5B0%5D)

An indefinite retention period: data is kept with no time limit.

Binary file attachments uploaded by the user. Download via `GET /v1/compliance/apps/chats/files/{claude_file_id}/content`.
File ID
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#message_list_response.files.items.id)

File creation timestamp

filename: stringî‚š
Display name of the file
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#message_list_response.files.items.filename)
md5: stringî‚š
Lowercase hex MD5 of the file's preferred downloadable variant, as recorded at upload time. Null when no stored hash is available.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#message_list_response.files.items.md5)
mime_type: stringî‚š
MIME type of the file's preferred downloadable variant (e.g. 'application/pdf')
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#message_list_response.files.items.mime_type)
size_bytes: numberî‚š
Size in bytes of the file's preferred downloadable variant, if known. Null for older files uploaded before size was recorded.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#message_list_response.files.items.size_bytes)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#message_list_response.files)
generated_files: array of object { id, filename, md5, 2 more } î‚š
Downloadable files the assistant created via tool use (e.g. PDF, spreadsheet, slide deck). Distinct from `files`, which are uploads attached to the message. Download via `GET /v1/compliance/apps/chats/generated-files/{claude_gen_file_id}/content`.
Opaque generated-file id, e.g. 'claude_gen_file_abc123'. Treat as an opaque string; the encoding may change without notice.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#message_list_response.generated_files.items.id)
filename: stringî‚š
Display name of the generated file
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#message_list_response.generated_files.items.filename)
md5: stringî‚š
Lowercase hex MD5 of the generated file, when available. Null when no stored hash is available.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#message_list_response.generated_files.items.md5)
mime_type: stringî‚š
MIME type reported by the tool that produced the file
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#message_list_response.generated_files.items.mime_type)
size_bytes: numberî‚š
Size in bytes of the generated file, when available. Null when the file has expired or size is not recorded.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#message_list_response.generated_files.items.size_bytes)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#message_list_response.generated_files)

####  Compliance APIAppsChatsFiles
##### [Get file metadata](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance/apps/chats/files/retrieve)
GET/v1/compliance/apps/chats/files/{claude_file_id}
##### [Delete file](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance/apps/chats/files/delete)
DELETE/v1/compliance/apps/chats/files/{claude_file_id}
##### [Download file content](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance/apps/chats/files/download)
GET/v1/compliance/apps/chats/files/{claude_file_id}/content
##### ModelsExpand Collapse 
FileRetrieveResponse object { id, claude_chat_ids, created_at, 5 more } î‚š
File metadata for GET /v1/compliance/apps/chats/files/{claude_file_id}.
Returns metadata only. Use the sibling `/content` endpoint to download the file bytes.
File ID
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#file_retrieve_response.id)
claude_chat_ids: array of stringî‚š
Chats this file is attached to. A file can be referenced by messages across multiple chats.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#file_retrieve_response.claude_chat_ids)
created_at: stringî‚š
File creation timestamp
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#file_retrieve_response.created_at)
filename: stringî‚š
Display name of the file, if set
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#file_retrieve_response.filename)
md5: stringî‚š
Lowercase hex MD5 of the file's preferred downloadable variant, as recorded at upload time. Null when no stored hash is available. The sibling `/content` endpoint also sets a `Content-MD5` header (base64 per RFC 1864) computed over the exact served bytes; when the two disagree, the header is authoritative.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#file_retrieve_response.md5)
message_ids: array of stringî‚š
Chat message IDs this file is attached to. A file can be referenced by multiple messages.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#file_retrieve_response.message_ids)
mime_type: stringî‚š
MIME type of the file's preferred downloadable variant (e.g. 'application/pdf'). May be null for files with no downloadable content (e.g. code-interpreter outputs).
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#file_retrieve_response.mime_type)
size_bytes: numberî‚š
Size in bytes of the file's preferred downloadable variant, if known
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#file_retrieve_response.size_bytes)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#file_retrieve_response)
FileDeleteResponse object { id, type } î‚š
Response for deleting a compliance file.
The ID of the file that was deleted
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#file_delete_response.id)
type: optional "claude_file_deleted"î‚š
Constant string confirming deletion
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#file_delete_response.type)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#file_delete_response)
####  Compliance APIAppsChatsGenerated Files
##### [Get Claude-generated file metadata](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance/apps/chats/generated_files/retrieve)
GET/v1/compliance/apps/chats/generated-files/{claude_gen_file_id}
##### [Download a Claude-generated file](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance/apps/chats/generated_files/download)
GET/v1/compliance/apps/chats/generated-files/{claude_gen_file_id}/content
##### ModelsExpand Collapse 
GeneratedFileRetrieveResponse object { id, claude_chat_id, created_at, 4 more } î‚š
Metadata for GET /v1/compliance/apps/chats/generated-files/{claude_gen_file_id}.
Returns metadata only. Use the sibling `/content` endpoint to download the bytes. The owning chat is included since the id is opaque; to find the specific message that produced the file, fetch `/v1/compliance/apps/chats/{claude_chat_id}/messages` and match on `generated_files[].id`.
Opaque generated-file id, e.g. 'claude_gen_file_abc123'.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#generated_file_retrieve_response.id)
claude_chat_id: stringî‚š
The chat this generated file belongs to
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#generated_file_retrieve_response.claude_chat_id)
created_at: stringî‚š
File creation timestamp, when available
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#generated_file_retrieve_response.created_at)
filename: stringî‚š
Display name of the generated file
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#generated_file_retrieve_response.filename)
md5: stringî‚š
Lowercase hex MD5 of the stored file. Null when no stored hash is available. The sibling `/content` endpoint also sets a `Content-MD5` header (base64 per RFC 1864) computed over the exact served bytes.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#generated_file_retrieve_response.md5)
mime_type: stringî‚š
MIME type of the stored file, when available
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#generated_file_retrieve_response.mime_type)
size_bytes: numberî‚š
Size in bytes of the stored file, when available
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#generated_file_retrieve_response.size_bytes)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#generated_file_retrieve_response)

ArtifactListResponse object { id, organization_id, organization_uuid, 6 more } î‚š
A hosted site published via Claude Code.
Artifact identifier (tagged ID)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#artifact_list_response.id)
Deprecatedorganization_id: stringî‚š
Organization identifier (tagged ID)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#artifact_list_response.organization_id)
organization_uuid: stringî‚š
Organization UUID this Artifact belongs to
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#artifact_list_response.organization_uuid)
owner_user_id: stringî‚š
Artifact owner's user identifier (tagged ID). Always set, so attribution survives after the owner's account is deleted or the owner leaves every organization under the parent.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#artifact_list_response.owner_user_id)
published_version_id: stringî‚š
Identifier of the version a non-owner viewer would render when `read_mode` permits them â€” the version the owner has pinned for non-owner readers if one is pinned, otherwise the owner's latest. When `read_mode` is `owner` no non-owner renders any version; the field still reports which version would be served were read_mode widened.
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#artifact_list_response.published_version_id)
read_mode: "org" or "owner" or "users"î‚š
Who can view this Artifact: only its owner, a named set of users, or every member of its organization
"org"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#artifact_list_response.read_mode%5B0%5D)
"owner"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#artifact_list_response.read_mode%5B1%5D)
"users"î‚š
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#artifact_list_response.read_mode%5B2%5D)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#artifact_list_response.read_mode)
updated_at: stringî‚š
Artifact last update timestamp, or null for Artifacts published before this field was recorded
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#artifact_list_response.updated_at)
user: object { id, email_address } î‚š
The user who owns a Code Artifact.
Fields that reference this type are null when the owner's account has been deleted or the owner is no longer a member of any organization under the parent organization.
User identifier (tagged ID)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#artifact_list_response.user.id)
email_address: stringî‚š
User's email address
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#artifact_list_response.user.email_address)
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#artifact_list_response.user)
versions: array of object { id, created_at, name } î‚š
Up to roughly 20 most-recently-published versions of this Artifact (older versions are not retained). Metadata only â€” use `GET /v1/compliance/code/artifacts/{artifact_id}/versions/{version_id}` to download a version's content.
Opaque version identifier

created_at: stringî‚š
When this version was published
[](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/compliance#artifact_list_response.versions.items.created_at)
name: stringî‚š
Artifact title at this version. Falls back to the version identifier when the title for an older version is no longer retained.
